campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary zoning rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
Extension of interim zoning ordinance #2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application being submitted for bridge planning and construction

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which community groups and events would receive city funding for the upcoming fiscal year. They approved specific amounts of money for different grant programs that help local organizations put on community events and activities around Saratoga.
keyTopics: Approved which organizations get city grant money and how much each receives
Set funding amounts for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants to clarify how they work

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting from March 25, 2026 focused on providing accessibility and transparency to residents. The council made sure people could watch and participate through multiple channels including cable TV, online streaming, and phone access, with live translations available in over 50 languages.
keyTopics: Live meeting access via cable, YouTube, and Zoom
Multi-language translation services (50+ languages)
Multiple phone dial-in options for residents without internet
Online streaming through city website and YouTube channel

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was scheduled for Wednesday, March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. Residents who needed to discuss zoning issues or property matters at that hearing should mark their calendars for the rescheduled date instead.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Next hearing rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning matters postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development programs that help local residents with housing and services. The group approved previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations seeking federal grant money to support affordable housing and human services in Sunnyvale.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications from local organizations
Affordable housing and human services program funding decisions
Approval of February meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching a program that gives businesses a chance to get their licenses in order without penalties, reaching out to shop owners at local shopping centers. The council also discussed plans to promote Cupertino businesses through summer events and shop local campaigns, while exploring partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events and offer tax incentives for new retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - giving businesses a penalty-free opportunity to comply with licensing requirements
Outreach to shopping centers to help business owners get registered
Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage residents to shop locally
Public-Private Partnerships for city event sponsorships
Tax incentives and economic development support for retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they addressed several postponed items and held two public hearings. The council discussed a weed abatement program to deal with overgrown weeds around the city, and gathered community input on how federal housing grant money should be spent in the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026 - clearing overgrown weeds on public/private properties
Federal HUD housing grant funding - public input on spending priorities for FY 2026/27
Multiple agenda items postponed or reconsidered from previous meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to approve several routine city matters. The council recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security services costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report addition to meeting agenda
